name: Auckland
summary: Auckland is New Zealand's biggest city, built on a skinny strip of land between two harbours. It is famous for its tall Sky Tower, its busy sailboat harbour, and the 53 sleeping volcanoes scattered all around it.
funFacts: Auckland is built on top of 53 volcanoes — the youngest one, Rangitoto Island, popped out of the sea only about 600 years ago.
The Sky Tower is 328 metres tall and has a glass floor near the top, so brave kids can stare straight down at the tiny streets below.
Auckland's nickname is the 'City of Sails' because more sailboats live here per person than almost anywhere else in the world.
More Pacific Islander people live in Auckland than in any other city on Earth, so you can hear lots of different languages around town.
The whole city sits on a strip of land less than two kilometres wide, with the Pacific Ocean on one side and the Tasman Sea on the other.
thingsToDo: Ride the lift up the Sky Tower and tiptoe across its see-through glass floor high above the city.
Catch a ferry to Rangitoto Island and hike the lava trail up its perfect volcano cone.
Climb to the grassy top of Mount Eden and peer down into its deep volcano crater.
Visit Auckland War Memorial Museum to see Maori treasures, a giant war canoe, and the volcano gallery.
Wander Viaduct Harbour to watch the yachts and eat fish and chips by the water.
Walk or drive across the huge Auckland Harbour Bridge for big views over the Waitemata.

wildlife.name: Kiwi
wildlife.description: Round, fluffy, flightless bird with a long beak that snuffles for bugs in the dark.

wildlife.name: Tuatara
wildlife.description: Spiky-backed reptile that lived alongside the dinosaurs and can live for over 100 years.

wildlife.name: Tui
wildlife.description: Glossy black songbird with a fluffy white throat tuft and a beautiful bubbly song.

wildlife.name: Orca
wildlife.description: Big black-and-white killer whale that visits the Hauraki Gulf to hunt stingrays.

localWord.meaning: Hello and 'be well' in te reo Maori, the language of New Zealand's first people.

images.alt: Auckland's tall Sky Tower glowing against the city's night sky.
The big steel Auckland Harbour Bridge stretching across the blue water.
Rangitoto Island's perfect volcano cone rising out of the sea.
View into the deep grassy crater of Mount Eden volcano in Auckland.
The grand stone Auckland War Memorial Museum on a sunny day.
Yachts and waterfront buildings around Auckland's Viaduct Harbour.
A North Island brown kiwi with its long pointy beak.
A spiky-backed tuatara reptile basking in the sun.
A glossy black tui bird with its fluffy white throat tuft, perched on a flax flower.
Two black-and-white orcas leaping out of the sea.
